verdict: 2026-07-02 14:18:14 PDT - Not sound as sketched. The high-level pattern is right: a controlled draft that mirrors `value` while unfocused and preserves user typing while focused is appropriate here, and no new dependency is warranted. The issue is the proposed ref-only focus signal plus a `[value]`-only effect; it misses an acceptance-case transition. I would keep the focus-time original-value ref for the live-attendee-count guard, but make focus itself explicit state and sync from `value` when `isFocused` becomes false or `value` changes.

findings.title: Ref-only resync misses unchanged clamped commits
findings.claim: The sketch only runs the draft-resync effect when `value` changes and uses `focusedValueRef.current != null` as hidden focus state. That fixes async hydration, and it does not directly clobber focused typing, but it fails when a negative edit clamps back to the same primitive prop value. Example: a rate field already at `0`, user types `-1`, blur commits, `clampNum(_, 0)` keeps `value` at `0`; blur only mutates a ref, so the `[value]` effect never runs and the displayed draft can remain `-1`. That violates AC3's requirement that committed negatives display as `>=0`.
findings.alternative: Use explicit `isFocused` state for the sync decision, while keeping `focusedValueRef` only as the original-focus-value guard that prevents stray blur from pinning live attendee tracking. On focus, set draft from `value`, set the ref, and set `isFocused(true)`. On blur, commit if changed and set `isFocused(false)`. Then sync with `useEffect(() => { if (!isFocused) setDraft(String(value ?? '')); }, [value, isFocused])`, or a functional `setDraft(current => displayDraft({ value, isFocused, currentDraft: current }))` helper to keep the pure node test seam.
findings.win: Covers hydration, live-count tracking while unfocused, focused typing preservation, and the zero/clamp display path without jsdom/testing-library, per-keystroke sanitizing, or an exhaustive-deps suppression.
